math_recheck: Both proofs verified verbatim against the primary appendix (Chelsea 1956, pp. 69-70, eqs (1)-(3): hypothesis P_{x1..xn}{f=0}=P{f=0}, P_B(A)=P(A), P_A(B)=P_B(A)P(B)/P(A)=P(B), unique extension => P(A)=P_A(A)=1) and the modern pi-system route against Norris 2.5.1; conditional/multiplication/Bayes/independence forms, Cauchy-Hadamard R=1/limsup|Xn|^(1/n), and the a.s.-constant jump argument all correct.
fixes: reasoning.md:65 corrected the limsup-measurability set identity: replaced the vestigial {limsup Xn<=x}=cap_m cup_n {sup_{k>=n}X_k<=x} (the cap_m did nothing without slack) with the standard correct form sup_{k>=n}X_k via {sup<=x}=cap_{k>=n}{X_k<=x} and {limsup Xn<=x}=cap_m cup_n {sup_{k>=n}X_k<=x+1/m}
caveat: German 1933 Grundbegriffe original unobtainable this run (refs/grundbegriffe_1933.pdf is an HTML 503 error page, not the source); load-bearing math instead verified against the authoritative Chelsea 1956 English translation of the same monograph (read this run, pp. 66-70) - does not affect correctness.
